title: Correcting Invalid Git Ref Name Validation

statement.markdown: Write a function to validate whether a given Git ref name is valid. A valid ref name is one that consists only of alphanumeric characters and hyphens. It cannot start with a hyphen and must be at least 1 character long.
statement.inputFormat: A string representing the ref name to be validated
statement.outputFormat: A boolean indicating whether the ref name is valid
statement.notes: The function should return false for invalid ref names and true for valid ones. It should handle edge cases and empty strings.

examples.explanation: Ref name starts with a hyphen
Ref name ends with a hyphen
Ref name contains a non-alphanumeric character

hints.text: Check if the ref name starts with a hyphen
Use a regular expression to check for invalid characters

editorial.title: Validating Git Ref Names
editorial.intuition: Use a regular expression to validate the ref name
editorial.optimalSolution: Use a regular expression and check for edge cases
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The regular expression covers all valid and invalid cases
editorial.dryRun: Test the regular expression with different input cases
